name: Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular desde Uña por el sendero del Escalerón a La Raya
region_intro: Jogging around Laguna de Uña offers routes through a landscape characterized by a karstic lagoon, surrounded by mountains and dense pine forests. The area features limestone terrain and dramatic cliffs, creating a unique high-mountain ecosystem where water, forest, and rock converge. This natural setting provides a tranquil backdrop for running, with paths that often follow the lagoon's edge or ascend to viewpoints. The region's diverse flora and fauna, including aquatic birds and native vegetation, add to the experience of these running routes. Best jogging routes around Laguna de Uña The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – running circular to Laguna de Uña, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the lagoon and its immediate surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya, a difficult 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the lagoon and impressive rock formations. Local runners also love the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular by Los Callejones de Las Majadas, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail leading through pine forests and unique rock formations, often completed in about 27 minutes. Jogging around Laguna de Uña is defined by its karstic lagoon, surrounding pine forests, and limestone cliffs. The network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain. The routes in Laguna de Uña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Laguna de Uña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Laguna de Uña?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Laguna de Uña, offering a range of distances and difficulties for all levels of runners.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Laguna de Uña offers easy running options perfect for beginners or families. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014398409/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-by-los-callejones-de-las-majadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular by Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.3-mile (3.7 km) trail that takes about 27 minutes to complete, winding through pine forests and unique rock formations.

Q: What are the distances of the running trails around Laguna de Uña?
A: The running trails around Laguna de Uña vary in length. You can find routes as short as 2.1 miles (3.4 km), like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014404502/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-running-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – running circular to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt;, up to longer, more challenging options such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint – Uña Lagoon loop from Uña&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 10.9 miles (17.6 km).

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running paths around Laguna de Uña are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014404502/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-running-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – running circular to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the lagoon.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on the running routes?
A: Laguna de Uña is a haven for wildlife. As you run, you might spot aquatic birds like moorhens and shoveler ducks, as well as dragonflies, nightingales, and even otters. The lagoon's unique ecosystem, with its reeds, poplars, and willows, supports a rich diversity of flora and fauna.

Q: Are there running routes with scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014406304/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-trail-run-circular-from-una-via-the-escaleron-path-to-la-raya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides impressive vistas of the lagoon and its dramatic rock formations. The Mirador de la Laguna (Lagoon Viewpoint) is also a key point of interest along the main circular path.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Laguna de Uña?
A: The running routes in Laguna de Uña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the tranquil atmosphere, the stunning natural backdrop of the karstic lagoon and pine forests, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there more challenging or advanced running trails available?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Laguna de Uña offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014406304/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-trail-run-circular-from-una-via-the-escaleron-path-to-la-raya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.0-mile (9.7 km) path with significant elevation changes, providing panoramic views and impressive rock formations.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the running paths?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover several points of interest. Along the main circular path around the lagoon, you'll find the Mirador de la Laguna with informative panels, the Hermitage of the Virgen del Espinar (a small deviation), and the historic fish farm and Old Hydroelectric Plant. The area is also a significant birdwatching spot.

Q: Is there parking available for runners at Laguna de Uña?
A: While specific parking areas for runners are not detailed, Laguna de Uña is an accessible location within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. It is generally well-equipped for visitors, suggesting parking facilities are available near the main access points to the lagoon and its trails.

Q: Is Laguna de Uña accessible by public transport for runners?
A: Information regarding direct public transport specifically to the running trailheads at Laguna de Uña is limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport options to Uña village, which is the closest settlement to the lagoon, and plan your journey from there.
tips: The trail continues along the forest track that runs along the edge of the calcareous walls and along the way there are natural viewpoints of the Júcar canyon, the Uña lagoon and the town of Uña. The trail PR-CU 37 passes through the last viewpoint and shortly after turns off the trail to the right to go down towards Uña via El Escalerón, a mountain path carved into the calcareous walls to connect the town of Uña with the mountains of the Wood Mill. The trail passes through a trout fish farm, the most common species in the rivers of the Serranía, and then through the River Fishing School.

The trail climbs the foot of the mountain that forms the pedestal of the stone walls and leads to La Raya, a completely horizontal natural step created by environmental erosion in the center of the calcareous walls. The views of the Uña lagoon, the town and the Júcar valley are magnificent. The path follows the ledge of La Raya to the left, looking for the natural way out of the path, but it is worth turning right and going completely through the narrow passageway to the end, a good place to watch the flight of vultures, eagles and with luck some Egyptian vulture that lives in these mountainous contours.

waypoints.name: The viewpoints of Uña and El Escalerón
